{{Quote|You already know more about this mission than you need to, Admiral.|[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]|<ref name="Empire 1">[[Empire 1|''Empire'' 1]]</ref>}}
'''Coy''' was a [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] and later an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], commanding officer of the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Mathayus]]''.

==Biography==
When [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] [[Declaration of a New Order|declared]] his [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Coy was made [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] of the [[Pallis Oversector]] and assumed the role of commander of the [[Sixteenth Army]].<ref name="TEGtW" /><ref name="SoR">''[[Strongholds of Resistance]]''</ref>

In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], the ''Mathayus'', under Admiral Coy's personal command, was assigned to transport [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] known as [[Dargulli/Legends|Dargulli]], because Vader was ordered by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to eliminate a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]-wielding [[Dargulli Dark Jedi|Force-sensitive]] on the planet. Aboard the bridge of the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], Coy asked Vader why the ''Mathayus'', one of the most powerful [[Warship/Legends|warships]] in the Imperial Starfleet, was required to arrest an outlaw on a backwater world. Irritated by Coy's straightforward question on sensitive matters, Vader punished the admiral for curiousness by using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to choke the admiral while asking Coy to state his questions to the Emperor. Afterward, Coy introduced his subordinates, First Officer [[Atali]] and Second Officer [[Dezsetes]]. Neither did Coy or Vader suspect that Dezsetes was, in fact, one of the conspirators in [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Trachta]]'s [[Anti-Sith conspiracy|scheme]] to overthrow the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] and that Dezsetes had orders to eliminate Vader aboard the ''Mathayus''.

During the mission for Dargulli, Vader was informed that the ''Mathayus'' had detected a distress signal of a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] ship near [[Yorn Skot]], a gas giant near Dargulli. The ''Mathayus'' pursued the Rebels until the ship was captured in a [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] and dragged to the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bay of the ''Mathayus''. However, the signal was falsified by Dezsetes and the spacecraft was rigged with explosives. First Officer Atali and several [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] boarded the ship, but unexpectedly the ship exploded, killing Atali and his boarding party. Despite Vader's presence in the hangar bay, he survived the explosion. Soon after, a Rebel ship hiding in the depths of Yorn Skot was detected by the ''Mathayus''. Coy, wishing to take revenge on the loss of his First Officer, requested Vader to permit the ''Mathayus'' to track down and destroy the ship. The ''Mathayus'' fired on the surface of the gas planet, forcing the ship to abandon its hiding place. As the Rebel ship attempted flee to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], it was annihilated by a [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] barrage coming from the ''Mathayus''.

When the ''Mathayus'' reached Dargulli and orbited the planet, Dezsetes intruded into Coy's private quarters. The treacherous second officer surprised Coy by drawing his [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and shooting the admiral in the shoulder. Then, Dezsetes shot Coy in the chest, killing him. After murdering his superior officer, Dezsetes became the commanding officer of the ''Mathayus'' and apparently covered up Coy's cause of death. The motive for Coy's assassination was to ensure that Dezsetes could eliminate Vader with a squad of clone stormtroopers, "programmed" loyal to the members of the conspiracy and transferred aboard the ''Mathayus''. Dezsetes felt that his plans were compromised by Coy's staunch loyalty to Vader.
